mechanism_narrative: Rising retail and food services sales signal robust consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings growth and pushes equity prices higher over the following month.
failure_condition: Rank IC over 3-year rolling window is below 0.03 or consumer spending changes fail to lead earnings revisions consistently.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rates above 9% indicate severe labor market slack that depresses wage growth and consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >50% of months when UNRATE exceeds 9%, or the negative signal fails to produce a significant average drawdown (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When both the federal funds rate and consumer price inflation are at depressed levels, the economy faces deflation risk that compresses profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Average forward return in triggered periods is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p>0.10) or correlation between the joint low signal and future returns is non-negative.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of extreme labor market slack (unemployment >8%), rising housing starts signal a turnaround in residential investment that precedes economic recovery and positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Housing starts increases in high-unemployment regimes do not lead to positive 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.05) over any two-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which boosts corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year correlation between INDPRO growth and forward 20-day returns is non-positive, or the signal is dominated by noise in 70% of months.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate above 0.5% from the near‑zero floor signals the beginning of monetary tightening, which raises the discount rate on equity cash flows and depresses stock prices within one week.
failure_condition: Average 5-day forward return after a rate hike above 0.5% is not negative, or the signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in payroll employment and retail sales confirm that consumer income and spending are both strengthening, a powerful combination that drives earnings growth and positive forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Joint positive readings do not lead to significantly higher returns than the unconditional mean (t-test p>0.05), or the interaction effect is absent in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: In regimes of elevated unemployment (above 8%), positive GDP growth is a stronger signal for equity returns because it reflects a structural improvement in the economy rather than a cyclical fluke, boosting confidence and forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Average 60-day forward return following GDP growth in high-unemployment regimes is not positive (one-sided test p>0.10), or the conditioning on UNRATE does not improve predictive accuracy over unconditional GDP growth.
